channel.title: Qinnovate Intel
channel.description: BCI security intelligence — neurotechnology, quantum computing, neuroethics, and adversarial research from the Quantified Interconnection Framework.

item.description: 24 devices, 38 brain regions, 13 physics constraints, 103 threat techniques. One searchable interface, one open API.

item.title: Neural Steganography Weaponization: How Invisible Display Flicker Controls Your Subconscious
item.description: A full threat model for SSVEP frequency hijack via imperceptible display flicker, chained with sensory-channel attacks from SAIL Lab, mapped through the Neural Impact Chain

item.title: Your Smart Glasses Are One Sensor Away From Hearing Your Thoughts
item.description: How a $38 muscle sensor turns consumer smart glasses into a subvocal surveillance device — and why no security framework models the threat

item.title: Your Brain Has Rights. We Counted Them.

item.title: The Neural Impact Chain: When Security Scores Predict Psychiatric Diagnoses
item.description: How we mapped 99 BCI attack techniques to DSM-5-TR diagnoses — and found that NISS scores correlate with the clinical categories

item.title: How Malicious Apps Derive Your Personal Biometrics — No Health Sensor Required
item.description: 28 ways consumer sensors extract biometric, physiological, and cognitive data without dedicated health hardware, and why the attack chain ends at your brain

item.title: TARA's First CVE: A 9-Year-Old Vulnerability Hiding in Every PC Speaker
item.description: How a BCI threat framework found an unfiled vulnerability in RealTek audio codecs that turns your headphones into microphones

item.title: One Researcher. One AI. A Missing Standard.
item.description: How human-AI collaboration produced the first open security framework for brain-computer interfaces

item.description: From the QIF Field Journal

item.title: Field Journal #001: Synesthesia, Neuroplasticity, and the QIF Unification Moment
item.description: From the QIF Field Journal

item.title: Apple BCI-HID Protocol Makes Brain Control a Native Input Method
item.description:
Apple's BCI Human Interface Device protocol, demonstrated with Synchron's Stentrode, makes neural signals a native input alongside touch and voice in iOS/iPadOS/visionOS. First public demo: ALS patient controlled iPad entirely by thought (Aug 2025). Meanwhile, OpenAI invested $250M in Merge Labs for non-invasive neural interfaces.
QIF Relevance: Billions of Apple devices become potential BCI endpoints. BCI-HID uses BLE transport with no PQ crypto. NSP device class tiers (T1/T2/T3) provide the compliance framework the MIND Act needs. QIF Band 3 and I0 threat models need Apple ecosystem-specific analysis.

item.title: OpenAI Invests $250M in Sam Altman's Brain-Computer Interface Startup Merge Labs
item.description:
OpenAI led a $250M funding round for Merge Labs, Sam Altman's non-invasive BCI startup building consumer-grade neural interfaces. The investment signals that the AI industry sees brain-computer interfaces as the next input modality — not a research curiosity, but infrastructure.
QIF Relevance: Consumer-grade BCIs at scale mean millions of unsecured neural endpoints. Non-invasive devices lower the attack barrier (no surgery = wider deployment = larger attack surface). QIF threat models for Bands S1-S3 and I0 need consumer device class analysis. NSP device tiers become critical for mass-market compliance.

item.title: NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards Finalized
item.description:
NIST has finalized three post-quantum cryptographic algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA). These standards will directly impact BCI encryption protocols at QIF Band 3.
QIF Relevance: QIF Band 3 (Protocol Security) specifications should reference these PQC standards for forward-secure neural data encryption.

item.description: What happens inside a quantum barrier — and why it might be the key to unhackable neural interfaces

item.title: Can Hackers Attack Quantum Computers Across Time and Space? The Truth Is Far More Terrifying

item.title: The OSI of Mind: Securing Human-AI Interfaces
item.description: The OSI of Mind: Securing Human-AI Interfaces

item.title: Synchron Stentrode Receives FDA Breakthrough Device Designation
item.description:
Endovascular BCI reaches another clinical milestone. The minimally invasive approach changes the attack surface analysis — no craniotomy means different physical security vectors.
QIF Relevance: QIF I0 (Neural Interface) threat model needs endovascular-specific vectors alongside traditional implanted devices.

item.title: UNESCO Publishes Recommendations on Neurotechnology Ethics
item.description:
First intergovernmental framework for neurotechnology governance. Addresses cognitive liberty, mental privacy, and neural data as a special category.
QIF Relevance: QIF Band 7 (Cognitive Sovereignty) aligns with UNESCO cognitive liberty principles. Framework validation opportunity.

item.title: First Adversarial EEG Attack Demonstrated at IEEE S&P
item.description:
Researchers demonstrate that adversarial perturbations in EEG signals can fool BCI decoders, causing misclassification of motor intent.
QIF Relevance: Directly validates QIF threat model: adversarial signal injection at Band 5 (Signal Processing). Coherence metric could detect these perturbations.
